summary: ICP/Internet Identity app with admin claim, registration forms (internal, partner, client), and role-based approval dashboard. Removing restriction that only admin can register users, replacing with open registration for all II-authenticated users while only #admin and #adminUser can approve/reject pending registrations.
architectural_notes: Backend is Motoko (main.mo) on ICP with Internet Identity authentication
Registration data stored in stable var with status: pending/approved/rejected
Three separate registration forms: internal, partner, client — each with distinct fields
known_issues: Build repeatedly failing when attempting large changes to main.mo; needs to be broken into smaller incremental steps

new_feature_requests.summary: Remove admin-only registration restriction in main.mo so all II-authenticated users can submit registration forms (internal, partner, client)
Only #admin and #adminUser can approve/reject pending registrations; registration data stored in stable var with initial status pending
For internal registrations, admin determines role at approval time; for partner and client, role is set by user at form submission
Users can only view their own registration status (pending/approved/rejected)

In backend/main.mo, remove any access-control guard on the user registration functions (for internal, partner, and client) that restricts registration to admin-only callers. After this change, any authenticated Internet Identity principal must be able to call these registration functions without a role check blocking them.
